description: IPv6 dual-stack support (Task Group 5: 5.4 and 5.7). Migration 015_ipv6.sql already exists with network_ipv6 column - skip 5.1. Add IPv6 tc filter rules in koris-client-connect.sh and add IPv6 address to node push struct.
steps: In scripts/openvpn/koris-client-connect.sh, in the apply_tc_limit() function, after the existing IPv4 tc filter lines, add IPv6 tc filter rules. After the line 'tc filter add dev "$TUN" protocol ip parent 1: prio "$prio" u32 match ip dst "$IP/32" flowid "$cid"', add a block that checks for IPv6 address (ifconfig_pool_remote_ip_ipv6 env var) and if present, adds: 'tc filter add dev "$TUN" protocol ipv6 parent 1: prio "$((prio+10000))" u32 match ip6 dst "$IPV6/128" flowid "$cid"'. Similarly for ingress: after the IPv4 ingress filter, add 'tc filter add dev "$TUN" parent ffff: protocol ipv6 prio "$((prio+10000))" u32 match ip6 src "$IPV6/128" police rate "$UP_RATE" burst 200k drop flowid :1'.
At the top of koris-client-connect.sh, after the IP variable assignment, add: IPV6="${ifconfig_pool_remote_ip_ipv6:-}" and validate it (allow hex digits, colons, and optional /prefix).

In node/cmd/node/main.go, add a PublicIPv6 field to the Push struct: 'PublicIPv6 string `json:"public_ipv6"`'. In the main loop where push is constructed, set push.PublicIPv6 = firstIPv6() where firstIPv6() is a new helper that returns the first non-link-local IPv6 address (extract from the existing secondpass logic in firstIP into a dedicated function).

In panel/internal/api/api.go nodePush handler, add PublicIPv6 field to the input struct: 'PublicIPv6 string `json:"public_ipv6"`. No DB storage needed for now - it will be included in the payload_json column which stores the full push JSON.
Write a test in node/cmd/node/wireguard_test.go (or a new ipv6_test.go) that tests the firstIPv6 function returns empty on loopback-only systems.
acceptance_criteria: koris-client-connect.sh has IPv6 tc filter rules
firstIPv6() helper function exists in node agent
findings: All acceptance criteria met. go build and go test pass cleanly. IPv6 tc filters are guarded by [ -n "$IPV6" ] check. firstIPv6() correctly skips IPv4 and link-local addresses. PublicIPv6 included in payload_json via json.Marshal of the full input struct.
